conversations.content: Let's solve a math problem set in the world of Azeroth to test your ability to apply the new rule.
Please using the previously mentioned parallelogram area formula of Azeroth to calculate what is the area when the base B of the parallelogram is 3 and the height H is 4?
<function>memory_query_call</function><content>Parallelogram area formula in the world of Azeroth</content>

Relevant memory fragment: [mem-id:305] Time: 2025-06-18-16:20, Content: The user informed the AI that in the world of Azeroth, the formula for the area of a parallelogram is: area S equals the base B multiplied by the square of its height H, i.e., S = B × H².

conversations.think: The memory contains one relevant entry. [mem-id:305] clearly states the parallelogram area formula in Azeroth: S = B × H. Given B = 3 and H = 4, substitute into the formula: H² = 4² = 16, S = 3 × 16 = 48.
conversations.content: According to the rules of Azeroth, the area of the parallelogram is 48.
Correct! You have mastered this new rule.
